method: Add domain-frontier staleness gate to open_lane.py. Measure current creation-maintenance asymmetry. Implement structural enforcement: BLOCK new DOMEX lanes for domains with >50 session stale frontiers, WARN for >30 sessions stale. Test gate with stale and recent domains.

pre_registration.hypothesis: FM-22 can be hardened from HIGH UNMITIGATED to MINIMAL (1 automated defense layer) by adding a creation-time maintenance gate to open_lane.py
pre_registration.threshold: Gate fires correctly for stale domains (>50s: BLOCK, >30s: WARN) and passes for recent domains (<30s)
pre_registration.falsification: Gate causes false positives that block legitimate work without escape valve

baseline.note: 81.4% domain frontier staleness is the primary creation-maintenance asymmetry signal. Lane lifecycle is healthy (91.8% merge rate). The problem is frontier maintenance, not lane closure.

implementation.mechanism: Domain frontier staleness check before lane creation
implementation.defense_layer_type: automated structural enforcement at creation time (L-601)

actual: Gate implemented and tested. linguistics (240s stale) correctly BLOCKED. catastrophic-risks (19s stale) correctly passed. 47% of domains would be BLOCKED, matching the 81.4% anxiety rate baseline.
diff: Expected simple WARN→BLOCK upgrade; actual implementation is a new domain-frontier-specific gate (not lane-based). Key insight: the asymmetry is in frontier maintenance, not lane lifecycle.
